articles.title: Pentagon seeks $80 billion funding boost to cover Iran war costs, additional bills
articles.description: The Pentagon is seeking $80 billion in additional funding as the cost of the Iran war rises and concerns grow over military resources.

articles.title: Taliban Takes War Across the Border: Kabul Claims Airstrikes Inside Pakistan
articles.description: The Afghan Taliban has claimed that its air force conducted strikes against alleged ISKP (Daesh) positions inside Pakistan's Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces.

articles.title: Mukesh Ambani’s big AI message at Reliance AGM: 'India must build, not just consume'
articles.description: At Reliance Industries’ 49th AGM, Ambani said Reliance Intelligence is entering its execution phase and will build AI infrastructure, platforms and services.

articles.title: 'Tough times never last, tough nations do,' says Mukesh Ambani: Key highlights from Reliance AGM 2026
articles.description: Ambani noted that the past six years have been among the most volatile and uncertain periods witnessed globally in several decades, marked by disruptions ranging from the pandemic to geopolitical conflicts, supply-chain shocks and economic turbulence.

articles.title: 'I Have Seen Brutal Things In Govt Boys School, Can't Even Talk About': Raakh Actor Akash Makhija
articles.description: Akash Makhija recollected an incident from his school where boys would scratch each other with tools in the geometry box.

articles.title: ‘Not our business what resolutions they pass’: Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge on Tamil Nadu’s stand on Mekedatu dam project
articles.description: Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge defended the Mekedatu dam project, stating Karnataka will pursue its interests legally. This comes after the Tamil Nadu Assembly passed a resolution opposing the dam, citing it violates existing legal provisions and Supreme Court judgments. Kharge emphasized Karnataka's aim to utilize surplus water without infringing on others' rights.

articles.title: NDA bags 19 out of 26: How close is the ruling coalition to Rajya Sabha two-third majority
articles.description: The BJP-led NDA has strengthened its Rajya Sabha presence, winning 19 of 26 recent seats to reach 150 members. This brings the alliance closer to a two-thirds majority, with potential further gains from upcoming bypolls and shifts in opposition alliances. However, regional parties and upcoming Uttar Pradesh elections could still influence the balance of power.

articles.title: India Pauses UPI-Alipay Plus Deal With China Over Security Concerns
articles.description: India has reportedly put the proposed UPI-Alipay Plus integration on hold as authorities continue to assess security, regulatory, and operational concerns. If approved, the partnership could allow Indian travelers to make seamless UPI payments at Alipay Plus-supported merchants overseas, particularly in China. However, government sources say no immediate decision is expected, with banking preparedness and cybersecurity checks remaining key priorities. Speaking on the development, Business Today's Karishma Assudani reported that discussions had taken place between Indian authorities and Alipay Plus representatives, but the proposal is still under detailed evaluation. Sources also clarified that there are currently no active discussions on bringing Apple Pay to India. Watch for the latest updates on India's evolving digital payments landscape.

articles.title: RIL AGM 2026: Mukesh Ambani hails PM Narendra Modi as longest-serving elected leader
articles.description: Ambani emphasized that Modi's political journey represents an unmatched feat in global governance. He lauded the Prime Minister for completing 25 uninterrupted years in public office, tracing back to his foundational tenure as Chief Minister of Gujarat starting in October 2001.

articles.title: What stands between Andy Burnham and No. 10 Downing Street?
articles.description: Andy Burnham has won a parliamentary seat. This move fuels speculation about him challenging Keir Starmer for Labour leadership. Several pathways exist for Burnham to potentially become prime minister. He could trigger an early contest or wait until after summer. Another politician might initiate a leadership race. An orderly transition is also possible if Starmer steps aside.

articles.title: PM Modi wishes LoP Rahul Gandhi on 56th birthday
articles.description: Narendra Modi: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended birthday greetings to Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, who turned 56 on Friday. Modi wished the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha good health and a long life in a post on X.

articles.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland abruptly called off after Vance withdraws
articles.description: The development comes after the White House said US Vice President JD Vance had withdrawn from the planned trip to Switzerland, where he was scheduled to meet Iranian negotiators.

articles.title: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke writes to PM Modi; flags student suicides, demands Pradhan's ouster
articles.description: Ahead of a protest, Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke urged PM Modi to provide Rs 1 crore compensation to families of students who allegedly died by suicide amid examination controversies. Dipke also reiterated the demand to sack Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, calling for accountability for alleged irregularities.

articles.title: Psychology says people who keep their cameras off in meetings may be doing this for a reason
articles.description: Video calls create unique social pressures, leading many to turn off their cameras to avoid self-consciousness. Research reveals "mirror anxiety" from seeing oneself constantly on screen contributes significantly to fatigue, particularly for women and introverts. This fatigue is linked to increased self-focused attention and negative feelings, suggesting camera-off is often a coping mechanism, not disengagement.

articles.title: BrahMos goes local: India successfully cuts Russian dependence with its first 100% homegrown missile warheads
articles.description: Earlier India imported boosters from Russia but after technology transfer in 2018, the production of indigenous warheads began in 2022.

articles.title: Abhishek Banerjee meets Speaker; BJP calls TMC row 'family grudge'
articles.description: BJP's Priyanka Tibrewal termed the TMC rebel issue a 'family's grudge' as Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla met Abhishek Banerjee. The meeting discusses the status of 20 rebel TMC MPs who want to merge with the NCPI, citing anti-defection law provisions.

articles.description: The US-Iran deal leaves Israel with fewer military options, unresolved threats from Hezbollah, and growing pressure from Washington to de-escalate.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can...” why purpose, not circumstances, often decides who keeps going when life becomes unexpectedly hard — learn the uplifting wisdom from the philosopher who challenged the world to think beyond comfort and hardship
articles.description: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.” We often wonder why some people break under pressure while others survive the hardest trials imaginable. The answer lies in a profound human truth about resilience and inner purpose. When you possess a powerful why to live for, your mind can endure any difficult how.

articles.title: ‘NYT will endorse a dead armadillo over a Republican’: Kennedy rebukes coverage of Iran peace deal
articles.description: Sen. John Kennedy unleashed a fiery defense of President Trump's Iran peace deal, accusing critics and media outlets of unfairly attacking the agreement before seeing the final terms. Kennedy mocked coverage of the deal, joking that some newspapers would 'endorse a dead armadillo over a Republican,' while arguing that Iran's leaders cannot be trusted without strict enforcement measures. The Louisiana Republican also delivered a blistering assessment of Tehran, warning that the Iranian government has a long history of deception and insisting that any agreement must include tough verification mechanisms. His comments come as the Trump administration faces mounting questions over sanctions relief, oil exports, and Iran's nuclear commitments.

articles.title: Where control weakens reform
articles.description: Rehabilitation homes: Juvenile justice rules shift focus from care to control weakening rehabilitation homes in India as staffing gaps reduced supervision and counselling under 2016 framework impacting reform outcomes in child care institutions India.

articles.title: Putin-Iran chopper deal with night vision tech stuns Trump amid peace deal? | Russia| US| Hormuz
articles.description: Just as President Trump thanks Vladimir Putin for Russia’s “neutrality” in the Iran war, Moscow quietly signs a major helicopter deal with Tehran—equipping Mojtaba’s forces with new, night‐vision‐capable aircraft. The move blindsides Washington and Israel, feeding a narrative that Putin has outmaneuvered Trump by talking peace while upgrading Iran’s capabilities behind his back.

articles.title: Who are Usain Bolt’s parents? Meet the couple who helped raise the fastest man alive
articles.description: Usain Bolt’s parents, Wellesley and Jennifer Bolt, were central figures in his journey from a small Jamaican village to global sporting greatness. Jennifer offered emotional support, while Wellesley provided discipline and guidance. They still live in Sherwood Content and run their local store, enjoying the rewards of the success their son worked so hard to achieve.

articles.description: Bhutto warned India over water supplies, while India dismissed the remarks as an attempt to divert attention from Pakistan's internal water crisis and terror concerns.

articles.title: 'TVK a democratic revolution’: After 55 years in CPI, veteran leader Mahendran joins Vijay’s party
articles.description: Veteran CPI leader C Mahendran has joined actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, citing a desire to strengthen communist ideology and public welfare. Mahendran, a seasoned politician with a five-decade record, believes TVK offers a platform for ordinary citizens and aims to eradicate corruption. He envisions the party as a mass movement, marking a significant shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: Pakistan PM's visit to Switzerland postponed due to electronic signing of US-Iran deal: Spokesman
articles.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has postponed a visit to Switzerland for a US-on Thursday. "The proposed visit has been postponed as the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has already been electronically signed, has entered into force, and is now under implementation," spokesman Mosharraf Zaidi said, adding Pakistan would support the next phase of several "technical-level" tracks. bur-ceg/lga

articles.description: For India, the US-Iran truce offers benefits on three fronts — energy security, maritime trade and regional connectivity.

articles.description: In a sharp counter to Bijapur MP Ramesh Jigjinagi’s statement, ‘why a Dalit should be concerned about RSS,’ Home Minister Priyank Kharge said on Thursday that he doesn’t fear threats by those “who don’t have constitutional recognition.”

articles.description: Only powerful actors can stop conflicts, good-hearted neutral mediators are passé. Peace comes when an involved party has the capacity to pressure, persuade, reassure, and set the terms of engagement

articles.description: Cross-voting: Congress blamed allies RJD and CPI(ML)L after losing a Rajya Sabha seat in Jharkhand amid alleged cross-voting. NDA-backed independent Parimal Nathwani won, boosting the BJP-led alliance's strength in the Upper House.
